nodes.label: adaptive-kv-quantization
nodes.summary: Treat layerwise, key/value, RoPE-aware, and hardware-aware KV bit allocation as experiments until reproduced in MLX.
nodes.rollback: no quality advantage over uniform KV quantization

nodes.label: audio-reference-conditioning-cache
nodes.summary: Cache repeated reference/ICL conditioning only with model/codec/quantization/speaker fingerprint keys.

nodes.label: audio-streaming-and-cache
nodes.summary: Use cache-aware streaming, overlap-add, and chunked codec/vocoder execution only when chunk boundaries preserve audio quality.

nodes.label: bf16-weight-cast
nodes.summary: Cast a controlled F32 MLX port to BF16 only when the exact target workload retains its declared quality window and the attested end-to-end measurement clears stability and noise gates.
nodes.rollback: quality output leaves the declared window

nodes.label: block-weight-streaming
nodes.summary: For repeated-block models that exceed unified memory, stream block weights from inspectable safetensors into a shared block only after eager parity is established.

nodes.label: cache-privacy-and-isolation
nodes.summary: Require namespace isolation, opt-out, padding/constant-time policy review, and leakage tests before cross-user cache reuse.

nodes.label: compile-stable-region
nodes.summary: Compile pure repeated regions after parity is established and shapes/state containers are stable.
nodes.validation_gate: compile count or cold/warm timing captured

nodes.label: content-prefix-cache-vlm
nodes.summary: Superseded by multimodal-content-prefix-cache; do not count this alias as a separate optimization or evidence line.

nodes.label: continuous-batching-serving
nodes.summary: Use only for concurrent serving where scheduler complexity is justified.

nodes.label: cuda-graphs-decode-capture
nodes.summary: Do not port. CUDA Graphs capture/replay is NVIDIA-specific; the portable MLX analog is mx.compile of the single decode step, which this skill already recommends.
nodes.validation_gate: Not applicable on Metal/MLX; use the mx.compile stable-region gate instead.
nodes.rollback: Always: this method is rejected for MLX and must not be adopted.

nodes.label: draft-model-speculation
nodes.summary: Try only when a compatible smaller draft model shares tokenizer/vocabulary and acceptance is high enough to offset draft cost.

nodes.label: eagle-medusa-mtp-drafters
nodes.summary: Use only when compatible trained drafter heads/artifacts and verification code exist for the target architecture.

nodes.label: fast-sdpa
nodes.summary: Try MLX fast SDPA when Q/K/V layout, GQA/MQA grouping, mask, scale, causal alignment, sinks, and dtype semantics match the source.

nodes.label: generic-audio-prefix-cache
nodes.summary: Keep generic audio prefix cache as a research experiment until a model-specific MLX-Audio cache path exists.

nodes.label: lazy-eval-boundaries
nodes.summary: Move host synchronization to intentional boundaries and avoid accidental NumPy conversion, printing, or scalar item extraction in hot loops.

nodes.label: moe-expert-dispatch-and-quantization
nodes.summary: After a loop oracle passes, move to MLX gather_mm/gather_qmm expert dispatch and quantize experts only after sensitivity tests.

nodes.label: moe-gate-up-fusion
nodes.summary: Treat fused MoE gate/up projection as an experimental patch, not baseline MLX support.
nodes.rollback: proposal not available in target runtime

nodes.label: moe-gather-and-expert-batching
nodes.summary: Use gather or grouped matmul primitives when expert routing creates large enough grouped matrix multiplies and duplicate/scatter semantics match.

nodes.label: multimodal-content-prefix-cache
nodes.summary: Use a media-aware block prefix cache only when restored cache coverage and media placeholder safety are proven.
nodes.validation_gate: media extra hashes include content and processing knobs

nodes.label: native-low-bit-weight-quantization
nodes.summary: Use MLX affine, mxfp4, mxfp8, or nvfp4 only where the target Linear path and conversion tooling support the mode.

nodes.label: prompt-lookup-ngram-speculation
nodes.summary: Consider as a low-memory experiment for copy-heavy workloads only after implementing a target MLX drafter.

nodes.label: prompt-prefix-cache
nodes.summary: Reuse immutable prefix state only when model, tokenizer/template, processor, adapter, quantization, positions, and cache format version match.

nodes.label: qwen3-tts-batch-generation
nodes.summary: Use Qwen3-TTS batch_generate for compatible concurrent TTS jobs after single-request quality is locked.
nodes.rollback: TTFB or P95 tail violates budget

nodes.label: spatial-grid-sample-kernel
nodes.summary: Use only when the source model actually depends on PyTorch grid_sample-style spatial or volume warping; first lock a readable MLX/NumPy reference, then consider a custom Metal kernel for the profiled warp.
nodes.rollback: unsupported source grid_sample mode is required

nodes.label: uniform-kv-quantization
nodes.summary: Start with official/proven uniform KV quantization before adaptive or rotated KV schemes.

nodes.label: video-input-budgeting
nodes.summary: Treat FPS/frame/pixel budgets as an explicit quality mode for video VLMs.

nodes.label: vision-feature-cache
nodes.summary: Use for repeated identical image/video inputs after keying by content hash and processor/projector revision.
nodes.rollback: warm outputs drift from cold outputs

nodes.label: visual-token-pruning-or-merge
nodes.summary: Do not recommend generic visual-token pruning unless the model-specific MLX implementation and quality probes exist.

nodes.label: golden-scenarios-all-families
nodes.summary: Golden scenarios now cover all 17 declared architecture families with reproducible synthetic fixtures.
nodes.validation_gate: Keep `tests.test_scenarios` green at full family coverage: each declared family must route correctly, select the expected runbook, prove source weight keys, catch a seeded parity bug, and include or exclude one relevant optimization.

nodes.label: graph-geometric-scientific
nodes.summary: Graph/geometric research now has first offline GCN-style message-passing routing coverage with a synthetic fixture and runbook. Point-cloud, neighbor/sampling, equivariant, protein, chemistry, and energy/force scientific ports remain validation backlog.
nodes.validation_gate: Keep the GCN-style graph-message-passing scenario green, then add point-cloud neighbor/sampling fixtures and one equivariant or energy-force fixture. Gates must include scatter/segment/reduce parity, permutation invariance, rotation/reflection equivariance probes, neighbor-list/radius graph fixtures, ragged batching, sparse/dense aggregation comparisons, units/symmetry/energy-force constraints, and task metrics such as OGB accuracy/ROC-AUC, ModelNet/ShapeNet accuracy or mIoU, molecular MAE/RMSE, and energy/force errors.

nodes.label: multi-source-research-sampling
nodes.summary: The deep research loop now embeds explicit sample targets for official docs, papers, Hugging Face, package registries, technical blogs, community leads, repositories, and repo-local audits in every assignment, and reports planned non-GitHub coverage before findings return.
nodes.validation_gate: Keep scaffold-only, offline-fixture, and local-executor research-loop tests green; planned sample targets must be reported separately from actual findings, stay review-only, and never auto-promote sources or execute remote model code.

nodes.label: non-generative-cv
nodes.summary: Non-generative CV now has first offline backbone/classifier routing coverage with a synthetic ResNet-style fixture and runbook. Dense/promptable vision, OCR, depth, pose, feature extraction on real checkpoints, and detection-style postprocessing remain validation backlog.
nodes.validation_gate: Keep the ResNet/CIFAR-style backbone/classifier scenario green, then add scoped fixtures before broader support claims: SAM-style prompt-to-mask, DINOv2 feature extraction, SegFormer semantic segmentation, Depth-Anything relative depth, and later DETR/Table Transformer boxes. Gates must include preprocessing parity, NCHW/NHWC conversion, logits/masks/boxes/depth/keypoint/string parity, coordinate and resize semantics, IoU/AP/Dice/AbsRel/RMSE/OKS/edit-distance/top-k metrics, and readable MLX or NumPy fallbacks for NMS, ROIAlign, and grid-sample-like paths.

nodes.label: source-formats-beyond-pytorch-hf
nodes.summary: Static intake now emits ONNX, GGUF, Flax/Orbax, TensorFlow SavedModel, Keras archive, Core ML package, and safetensors-checkpoint manifests, with conservative operator/layer coverage reports for ONNX, TensorFlow, and Keras plus deterministic weight-map shape coverage for ONNX initializers and GGUF tensor tables. Broader checkpoint-only repo fixtures remain.
nodes.validation_gate: Static manifest extraction without import or execution: keep ONNX ModelProto/opset/initializer/external-data inspection, GGUF header/metadata/tensor table coverage, Flax/Orbax tree metadata, TensorFlow SavedModel and Keras archive signatures/config, Core ML package metadata, ONNX/TensorFlow/Keras static coverage reports, safetensors-only checkpoint holds, ONNX/GGUF source-format weight-map validation, and no silent fallback to manual routing green; add broader checkpoint-only repo fixtures.

nodes.label: structured-timeseries-recsys
nodes.summary: Structured/time-series research now has first offline forecasting routing coverage with a synthetic Time-Series-Transformer-style fixture and runbook. Tabular/skops, deep tabular, text ranking, and recommender subfamilies remain validation backlog.
nodes.validation_gate: Keep the time-series forecasting scenario green, then create separate fixtures for tabular/skops or deep tabular, cross-encoder text ranking, and later recommender subfamilies. Gates must include scaler/normalizer parity, lag construction, observed-mask handling, context/prediction split, quantile or forecast-patch parity, known-future covariate leakage checks, categorical vocab and missing-value policy, jagged offsets/lengths and embedding pooling parity, pair-score parity, candidate-id versus score-based retrieval checks, NDCG/AUC/top-k/forecast-error task gates, loader family, remote-code risk, and rollback condition.

nodes.label: top1000-contributor-long-tail-rescreening
nodes.summary: The current MLX contributor refresh requested 1000 contributors, captured 262 linked contributors and 268 anon=true author buckets through 2026-07-08. Collection remains source-selection evidence only; repository/code-search rescreening and implementation review remain open.
nodes.validation_gate: Refresh contributors with `scripts/collect_contributors.py`, following GitHub Link headers until no rel="next" remains or the explicit cap is reached; record requested count, linked count, anon=true count without raw anonymous identities, status codes, ETags, Last-Modified headers, rate-limit state, repository/code search total_count and incomplete_results, replay missed queries, inspect newly matched repos by architecture/source-format family, and record each result as adopted, held, rejected, or backlog.

nodes.label: training-as-port-target
nodes.summary: Official MLX autograd, Module, optimizer, compile, memory, checkpoint, and LoRA references now define a concrete training-port contract, but no local training parity fixture has been run.
nodes.validation_gate: Add a tiny training fixture before support claims: scalar loss parity, selected gradient parity, trainable/frozen parameter membership, tiny overfit, optimizer state round-trip, exact checkpoint resume with optimizer config, scheduler, random state, data cursor and accumulation remainder, deterministic seed replay, train/eval mode checks, adapter key/shape manifest, adapter merge/fuse parity, eager-vs-compiled state-capture parity, gradient-checkpoint on/off parity, active/cache/peak memory logs, and memory graph-retention checks.

nodes.label: AffectCodec: Emotion-Preserving Neural Speech Codec with Block-Diagonal Residual FSQ
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: Neural speech codecs have become the discrete interface between raw audio and speech language models, yet they remain optimized primarily for acoustic reconstruction fidelity, which leaves emotion-relevant cues vulnerable to being discarded during quantization, limiting the affective capacity of downstream models. We trace this degradation to two mechanisms: reconstruction-driven bit allocation under limited bitrate and cross-stream leakage in concatenation-based codecs, where acoustic gradients can overwrite nominally emotion-reserved dimensions. We propose AffectCodec, an emotion-preserving neural speech codec built on Block-Diagonal Residual Finite Scalar Quantization (BD-RFSQ). By imposing block-diagonal input and output projections over emotion and acoustic subspaces, BD-RFSQ transfor

nodes.label: Agent-Assisted Side-Channel Attacks on Non-Prefix KV Cache in RAG
nodes.summary: Modern Large Language Model (LLM) serving engines increasingly rely on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and non-prefix Key-Value (KV) cache fusion to accelerate long-context, multi-tenant inference. While existing KV cache side-channel attacks require strict linear prefix alignment--rendering them ineffective against real-world RAG queries that contain unique, user-specific private prefixes--we uncover a critical class of structural vulnerabilities inherent to chunk-aware memory scheduling. We demonstrate that the deterministic micro-architectural mechanisms used to align and fuse disjoint memory chunks inadvertently leak a continuous "Step-Wave" timing signature. Exploiting this physical observation, we introduce SpliceLeak, the first end-to-end side-channel attack targeting non-prefi

nodes.label: Attention based on-device streaming speech recognition with large speech corpus
nodes.summary: In this paper, we present a new on-device automatic speech recognition (ASR) system based on monotonic chunk-wise attention (MoChA) models trained with large (> 10K hours) corpus. We attained around 90% of a word recognition rate for general domain mainly by using joint training of connectionist temporal classifier (CTC) and cross entropy (CE) losses, minimum word error rate (MWER) training, layer-wise pre-training and data augmentation methods. In addition, we compressed our models by more than 3.4 times smaller using an iterative hyper low-rank approximation (LRA) method while minimizing the degradation in recognition accuracy. The memory footprint was further reduced with 8-bit quantization to bring down the final model size to lower than 39 MB. For on-demand adaptation, we fused the Mo

nodes.label: AugCodec: A Low-Bitrate Disentangled Neural Speech Codec via Data Augmentation
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: We propose AugCodec, a low-bitrate disentangled neural speech codec that leverages data augmentation to decompose speech into three distinct components: semantic, speaker, and prosody tokens. Specifically, we employ tailored augmenta tion strategies to transform speech into distinct variants, each serving as input for extracting tokens that preserve the target attribute while suppressing others. This disentanglement strategy enables substantial reduction in token rate. Further more, we introduce an augmentation loss that aligns semantic encoder outputs between source and voice-converted speech, encouraging speaker-agnostic embeddings while mitigating the acoustic mismatch induced by voice conversion. Experiments on LibriSpeech test-clean demonstrate that AugCodec significantly outperforms

nodes.label: Benchmarking On-Device Machine Learning on Apple Silicon with MLX
nodes.summary: The recent widespread adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) and machine learning in general has sparked research interest in exploring the possibilities of deploying these models on smaller devices such as laptops and mobile phones. This creates a need for frameworks and approaches that are capable of taking advantage of on-device hardware. The MLX framework was created to address this need. It is a framework optimized for machine learning (ML) computations on Apple silicon devices, facilitating easier research, experimentation, and prototyping. This paper presents a performance evaluation of MLX, focusing on inference latency of transformer models. We compare the performance of different transformer architecture implementations in MLX with their Pytorch counterparts. For this research

nodes.label: ELiTeFormer: An Efficient Transformer for FPGAs
nodes.summary: Transformer blocks are prevalent in large language model (LLM) but present deployment challenges due to their challenging computational and memory demands. While prior work has typically optimized attention mechanisms or feed-forward networks (FFNs) separately, few hardware (HW) architecture have jointly addressed both components with co-designed hardware acceleration. We present ELiTeFormer (Efficient Linear Ternary Transformer), the first Transformer model architecture that unifies hybrid linear attention with ultra-low-precision (ternary) linear projections, specifically co-designed for field-programmable gate array (FPGA) deployment. ELiTeFormer achieves 10x model weight compression and 12.8x key-value (KV) cache compression compared to LLaMA 3, while maintaining competitive accuracy (

nodes.label: FlashLabs Chroma 1.0: A Real-Time End-to-End Spoken Dialogue Model with Personalized Voice Cloning
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: Recent end-to-end spoken dialogue systems leverage speech tokenizers and neural audio codecs to enable LLMs to operate directly on discrete speech representations. However, these models often exhibit limited speaker identity preservation, hindering personalized voice interaction. In this work, we present Chroma 1.0, the first open-source, real-time, end-to-end spoken dialogue model that achieves both low-latency interaction and high-fidelity personalized voice cloning. Chroma achieves sub-second end-to-end latency through an interleaved text-audio token schedule (1:2) that supports streaming generation, while maintaining high-quality personalized voice synthesis across multi-turn conversations. Our experimental results demonstrate that Chroma achieves a 10.96% relative improvement in speak

nodes.label: FlexiCodec: A Dynamic Neural Audio Codec for Low Frame Rates
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: Neural audio codecs are foundational to speech language models. It is expected to have a low frame rate and decoupled semantic and acoustic information. A lower frame rate codec can reduce the computational cost of speech language models by shortening the sequence length. Recent studies have developed 12.5Hz low-frame-rate audio codecs, but even lower frame rate codecs remain underexplored. We find that a major challenge for very low frame rate tokens is missing semantic information. This paper introduces FlexiCodec to address this limitation. FlexiCodec improves semantic preservation with a dynamic frame rate approach and introduces a novel architecture featuring an ASR feature-assisted dual stream encoding and Transformer bottlenecks. With dynamic frame rates, it uses less frames at info

nodes.label: Fractal KV-Cache Archives: Lossless Symbolic Storage with In-Place Retrieval for Long-Context LLM Inference
nodes.summary: The key-value (KV) cache dominates the memory cost of long-context autoregressive inference, and a growing body of work compresses it through quantization, eviction, or offloading. We study a complementary question: once a position's KV state has been quantized to codebook indices, how should the resulting symbol stream be stored, and can the storage layer do more than store? A family of contractive iterated-map codes that serialize a symbol sequence into a sequence of low-dimensional real vectors is revisited, and it is shown that they form a natural archive format for a quantized KV cache with the following features. The method provides exactly the access pattern a growing cache requires. It is lossless, it runs in linear time, and supports O(1) random access and O(1) amortized append. A

nodes.label: HybridCodec: Fast Dual-Stream, Semantically Enhanced Neural Audio Codec
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: The popularity of neural audio codecs as speech tokenizers has surged with the advent of Multimodal Large Language Models. New codec architectures with semantic and acoustic disentanglement have emerged. There are two main approaches to introduce semantic information into codec models: one distills semantic information from SSL representations into the first RVQ layer, while the other maintains separate streams for semantic and acoustic features. We propose HybridCodec, a unified architecture that combines both paradigms. It employs separate semantic and acoustic branches while distilling SSL representations into the semantic stream. This design ensures strong disentanglement without requiring an SSL model during inference. HybridCodec shows superior semantic specialization (RVQ-1) on in-d

nodes.label: KernelCraft: Benchmarking for Agentic Close-to-Metal Kernel Generation on Emerging Hardware
nodes.query: Metal GPU custom kernel machine learning
nodes.summary: New AI accelerators with novel instruction set architectures (ISAs) often require developers to manually craft low-level kernels, a time-consuming and error-prone process that does not scale across hardware targets. This delays emerging hardware platforms from reaching the market. While prior LLM-based code generation has shown promise in mature GPU ecosystems, it remains unclear whether agentic LLM systems can quickly produce valid and efficient kernels for emerging hardware with new ISAs. We present KernelCraft: the first benchmark for evaluating an LLM agent's ability to generate and optimize low-level kernels for customized accelerators through a function-calling, feedback-driven workflow. We evaluate agent performance across three emerging accelerators on more than 20 machine-learning

nodes.label: Lynx: Progressive Speculative Quantization for accelerating KV Transfer in Long-Context Inference
nodes.summary: Long-context inference is increasingly common in large language model (LLM) serving, driven by retrieval-augmented generation and agentic systems. In disaggregated inference, these workloads require transferring large Key-Value (KV) caches across the network, where decoding cannot begin until the transfer completes. Recent KV quantization techniques reduce data volume and alleviate this bottleneck, but existing schemes fail to achieve both low network-exposed latency and high inference accuracy. We challenge the assumption that the KV cache is an indivisible unit that must be fully received before use. We leverage the observation that different bits in the KV cache contribute unequally to attention computation and inference precision: the most significant bits capture the coarse structure

nodes.label: Native LLM and MLLM Inference at Scale on Apple Silicon
nodes.summary: The growing adoption of Apple Silicon for machine learning development has created demand for efficient inference solutions that leverage its unique unified memory architecture. However, existing tools either lack native optimization (PyTorch MPS) or focus solely on text models, leaving multimodal workloads underserved. We present vllm-mlx, a framework for efficient LLM and MLLM inference on Apple Silicon built natively on MLX. For text models, we achieve 21\% to 87\% higher throughput than llama-cpp across models ranging from Qwen3-0.6B to Nemotron-30B, while providing continuous batching that scales to 4.3x aggregate throughput at 16 concurrent requests. For multimodal models, we introduce content-based prefix caching that eliminates redundant vision encoding by identifying identical ima

nodes.label: Probing neural audio codecs for distinctions among English nuclear tunes
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: State-of-the-art spoken dialogue models (Défossez et al. 2024; Schalkwyk et al. 2025) use neural audio codecs to "tokenize" audio signals into a lower-frequency stream of vectorial latent representations, each quantized using a hierarchy of vector codebooks. A transformer layer allows these representations to reflect some time- and context-dependent patterns. We train probes on labeled audio data from Cole et al. (2023) to test whether the pitch trajectories that characterize English phrase-final (nuclear) intonational tunes are among these patterns. Results: Linear probes trained on the unquantized latents or some of the associated codewords yield above-chance accuracy in distinguishing eight phonologically specified nuclear tunes with monotonal pitch accents (top average test accuracy (T

nodes.label: ProsoCodec: Prosody-Oriented Speech Codec for Voice Conversion
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: Neural speech codecs efficiently compress speech and have become a foundation for speech generation, but they are typically learned as holistic representations that intertwine linguistic content, speaker identity, and prosody. While this design is effective for zero-shot voice cloning, it hinders downstream tasks that require prosody preservation or transfer, such as voice conversion. To address this, we introduce ProsoCodec, a prosody-oriented speech codec that models prosody as a conditional residual rather than as a disentangled stream. Specifically, by conditioning both the encoder and decoder on text and speaker embeddings as prefix tokens, the discrete bottleneck is encouraged to capture prosodic variation not explained by content and speaker. To further preserve prosody, we use the

nodes.label: Pushing the Limits of On-Device Streaming ASR: A Compact, High-Accuracy English Model for Low-Latency Inference
nodes.summary: Deploying high-quality automatic speech recognition (ASR) on edge devices requires models that jointly optimize accuracy, latency, and memory footprint while operating entirely on CPU without GPU acceleration. We conduct a systematic empirical study of state-of-the-art ASR architectures, encompassing encoder-decoder, transducer, and LLM-based paradigms, evaluated across batch, chunked, and streaming inference modes. Through a comprehensive benchmark of over 50 configurations spanning OpenAI Whisper, NVIDIA Nemotron, Parakeet TDT, Canary, Conformer Transducer, and Qwen3-ASR, we identify NVIDIA's Nemotron Speech Streaming as the strongest candidate for real-time English streaming on resource-constrained hardware. We then re-implement the complete streaming inference pipeline in ONNX Runtime

nodes.label: Quantization for OpenAI's Whisper Models: A Comparative Analysis
nodes.summary: Automated speech recognition (ASR) models have gained prominence for applications such as captioning, speech translation, and live transcription. This paper studies Whisper and two model variants: one optimized for live speech streaming and another for offline transcription. Notably, these models have been found to generate hallucinated content, reducing transcription reliability. Furthermore, larger model variants exhibit increased latency and pose challenges for deployment on resource-constrained devices. This study analyzes the similarities and differences between three Whisper models, qualitatively examining their distinct capabilities. Next, this study quantifies the impact of model quantization on latency and evaluates its viability for edge deployment. Using the open source LibriSpe

nodes.label: RaBitQCache: Rotated Binary Quantization for KVCache in Long Context LLM Inference
nodes.summary: Long-context Large Language Model inference is severely bottlenecked by the massive Key-Value (KV) cache, yet existing sparse attention methods often suffer from static fixed-budget (Top-k) retrieval or rely on proxy scores that are computationally expensive and biased. To address these limitations, we propose RaBitQCache, a novel sparse attention framework that utilizes randomized rotated binary quantization and high-throughput binary-INT4 arithmetic to efficiently estimate attention weights. Our proxy score serves as an unbiased estimator with a proven error bound, enabling adaptive Top-p retrieval that dynamically adjusts the token budget based on actual attention sparsity. We further implement a hardware-aware system with asynchronous pipelining and lazy updates to mask overhead. Evalu

nodes.label: RoPE-Aware Bit Allocation for KV-Cache Quantization
nodes.summary: Existing low-bit KV-cache quantizers often treat each cached key as a flat vector. Under RoPE, however, a key's contribution to a future attention logit decomposes into a position-dependent sum over two-dimensional frequency blocks. This makes key-cache quantization a block-wise bit-allocation problem: high-energy RoPE blocks are more sensitive to quantization error and should receive more bits. We introduce Block-GTQ, a RoPE-aware bit allocator for key-cache quantization built on TurboQuant-MSE(TQ-MSE). For each layer and KV head, Block-GTQ computes a label-free energy score for each RoPE block and greedily allocates integer bit widths by marginal gain. Under matched K/V bit budgets, Block-GTQ better preserves RoPE query-key logits on a ten-model diagnostic panel, cutting per-layer MAE by

nodes.label: StreamVoiceAnon+: Emotion-Preserving Streaming Speaker Anonymization via Frame-Level Acoustic Distillation
nodes.query: neural audio codec speech tokenizer streaming
nodes.summary: We address the challenge of preserving emotional content in streaming speaker anonymization (SA). Neural audio codec language models trained for audio continuation tend to degrade source emotion: content tokens discard emotional information, and the model defaults to dominant acoustic patterns rather than preserving paralinguistic attributes. We propose supervised finetuning with neutral-emotion utterance pairs from the same speaker, combined with frame-level emotion distillation on acoustic token hidden states. All modifications are confined to finetuning, which takes less than 2 hours on 4 GPUs and adds zero inference latency overhead, while maintaining a competitive 180ms streaming latency. On the VoicePrivacy 2024 protocol, our approach achieves a 49.2% UAR (emotion preservation) with

nodes.label: UltraQuant: 4-bit KV Caching for Context-Heavy Agents
nodes.summary: Context-heavy agents place unusual pressure on the key-value (KV) cache: long prefixes are reused across many short turns, while concurrency determines whether the serving system can keep GPUs utilized. We study 4-bit KV-cache compression for this setting, using TurboQuant-style rotation and codebook quantization as a quality anchor and vLLM FP8 KV caching as the deployment anchor. We report three contributions. First, we frame 4-bit KV caching around multi-round agent workloads where task quality, cache residency, and serving throughput must be measured jointly. Second, we describe the practical design choices needed to make the 4-bit path robust, including asymmetric K/V treatment, Walsh-Hadamard rotation, QJL removal, and block-scale variants. Third, we present serving optimizations on

nodes.label: What to Keep, What to Forget: A Rate--Distortion View of Memory Compaction in LLMs and Agents
nodes.summary: Large language models, and the agents built on them, spend an ever-growing share of their compute and memory on remembering: caching attention keys and values, carrying long prompts, maintaining recurrent state, and storing what happened in previous turns and sessions. Because none of this memory is free, four largely separate research communities have each learned to compact it. They evict or quantize the KV cache, prune or distill prompts, bound architectural state, and consolidate agent memory. We argue that these are instances of one problem: a rate--distortion decision about what context-derived information to retain versus discard, at what fidelity, under a resource budget, so as to preserve downstream task utility. We make this lens precise with a single compaction objective and a l

nodes.label: block-weight-streaming
nodes.summary: Add a proven-mlx-port optimization method for block weight streaming from inspectable safetensors, scoped to repeated-block models that exceed unified-memory budget.
nodes.validation_gate: Block-count/key coverage, streamed-vs-eager parity on a tiny fixture, eviction/reload test, peak-memory measurement, and fallback eager path.
nodes.rollback: Disable streaming if outputs drift, block key coverage is incomplete, eviction reloads stale weights, or disk/page-fault overhead erases the memory benefit.

nodes.validation_gate: Coordinate order, align_corners, padding mode, interpolation, dtype, and layout parity against NumPy/Torch; unsupported-mode declaration; pure-MLX fallback; end-to-end benchmark.
nodes.rollback: Disable or hold if reflection/nearest/backward semantics are required, parity drifts, layout transposes dominate, or the model-level benchmark does not improve.

nodes.label: layer-eval-watchdog-guard
nodes.summary: The skill already recommends deliberate lazy-evaluation boundaries; add a note in runtime guidance, but do not create a separate optimization method without a repo-local watchdog reproducer.

nodes.label: layer-range-network-sharding
nodes.summary: The implementation is explicitly a demo/start point and lacks enough parity, failure, cancellation, scheduler, and security evidence to become supported porting guidance.

nodes.label: mlx-onnx-export-adjacent
nodes.summary: Useful adjacent ecosystem context, but not a model-to-MLX porting technique for this skill.

nodes.label: operation-benchmark-hygiene
nodes.summary: The skill already separates microbenchmarks from end-to-end claims and requires hardware/workload metadata; no new supported optimization follows from operation tables alone.

nodes.label: paged-kv-and-gather-qmm-serving
nodes.summary: Existing paged KV, continuous batching, and MoE gather/qmm guidance already requires block-boundary, fragmentation, cancellation, sorted/unsorted, and loop-oracle checks.

nodes.label: rwkv-ssm-conformer-prototypes
nodes.summary: The current SSM/recurrent/ASR runbooks already require full-sequence versus recurrent parity, state tests, and source oracle capture; these prototypes do not justify new supported guidance.

nodes.label: synthetic-moe-assembly
nodes.summary: This is architecture creation/fine-tuning, not a source-preserving port optimization. It should remain a lossy/product-mode experiment unless a task explicitly asks to train or publish a new MoE.

nodes.label: Whisper ASR route
nodes.summary: Whisper-family ASR has working Apple-platform routes, but the advisor should separate Core ML or example support from generic MLX optimization claims.
nodes.validation_gate: Run feature parity, decoded text/timestamp checks, WER/CER, real-time factor, and memory benchmark for the target audio distribution.

nodes.label: Audio and TTS partial routes
nodes.summary: MLX-Audio and Qwen3-TTS evidence can guide TTS/audio candidates, but each model still needs duration, vocoder, reference-audio, and perceptual quality gates.
nodes.validation_gate: Run audio frontend parity, duration/vocoder checks, perceptual or task metric, RTF, and peak-memory benchmark.

nodes.label: MLX-LM decoder route
nodes.summary: For common decoder LLM families, start with MLX-LM conversion, loading, generation, cache, and quantization before writing a bespoke port.
nodes.validation_gate: Run static intake, source logits/cache parity, and target workload benchmark before publishing a numeric improvement.

nodes.label: Diffusion and flow partial routes
nodes.summary: Diffusion and flow models have MLX examples and contributor ports, but outcomes are model-specific and cannot be promoted to a top-model-wide route without scheduler and conditioning parity.
nodes.validation_gate: Run scheduler, latent, conditioning, image/audio quality, peak memory, and wall-time gates per model.

nodes.label: Encoder and embedding gap
nodes.summary: Many top downloaded models are embedding, reranker, BERT, CLIP, or feature-extraction models; this skill has routing gates but not a broad proven MLX outcome matrix for them yet.
nodes.validation_gate: Add model-class fixtures for pooling, preprocessing, pair scoring, logits/features, and task metric parity before calling these covered.

nodes.label: Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct local worked port
nodes.summary: Revision 7ae557604adf67be50417f59c2c2f167def9a775 completed the offline static-intake, Torch-oracle, standalone MLX scaffold, schema-2 weight conversion, 29-rung parity, exact greedy-generation, MLX-LM cross-check, and schema-2 receipt flow on Apple M4 Pro.
nodes.validation_gate: Add multiple prompts and boundary lengths, full-versus-incremental logit/cache parity, batch coverage, an independently attested runner, and a longer BF16 task-quality evaluation before widening the claim.

nodes.label: Time-series and structured gap
nodes.summary: Top downloaded Chronos and structured/ranking models should not inherit LLM optimization guidance; they need task-specific preprocessing and metric gates.
nodes.validation_gate: Add scaler, lag, mask, quantile, leakage, pair-score, NDCG/AUC/top-k, and forecast-error fixtures.

nodes.label: MLX-VLM multimodal route
nodes.summary: For Qwen-VL, LLaVA-style, Pixtral, Gemma vision, and related image-text models, use MLX-VLM before inventing a new multimodal serving path.
nodes.validation_gate: Run processor tensor parity, text+media output parity, cache key tests, memory accounting, and repeated-input benchmark.
